content: We can reframe curiosity-driven reinforcement learning agents' intrinsic reward signals as a built-in online meta-learning memory consolidation mechanism, where unexpected, high-curiosity experiences naturally act as 'prioritized rehearsal samples' to mitigate catastrophic forgetting of previously learned task representations. This eliminates the need for separate, computationally expensive external replay buffers that are standard in current catastrophic forgetting mitigation methods for online meta-learning.
source_topics: Online meta-learning memory consolidation with catastrophic forgetting mitigation
trigger_topic: Online meta-learning memory consolidation with catastrophic forgetting mitigation
